briefing.headline: Rig up a Texas Rig — aggressive post-spawn approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Texas Rig is a strong choice for post-spawn with clear water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Peg the sinker on baits with a lot of action (craw-style); leave it unpegged on gliding-action baits like tubes so the weight slides away for better glide.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Peg the sinker on baits with a lot of action (craw-style); leave it unpegged on gliding-action baits like tubes so the weight slides away for better glide.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, creek channel, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Johnston: There probably isn't a better bait for smallmouths than the tube since it imitates everything a smallmouth loves to eat, from perch to gobies to crawfish. If the bottom is clean, you can't b
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, creek channel, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: Neko-rigged creature baits are 'deadly springtime bait around the spawn.' In clear water, most fish bite on the initial fall — if they're on a bed, the bait lands next to them and they eat it immediately. Works for both largemouth and smallmo...

Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal post-spawn bass are transitioning and aggressive. Males guard fry in 2-6ft while females roam 5-12ft feeding heavily. Target both shallow fry-guarders and roaming post-spawn females near points.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
